content.problemStatement:
There is an **empty bus** with MMM seats and a total of NNN people, numbered from 111 to NNN. Everyone is currently outside the bus. You are given a sequence of QQQ events of the following form.



+ i+\ i+ i :  It denotes that the person iii enters the bus.




− i-\ i− i : It denotes that the person iii leaves the bus.



It is guaranteed in the input that each person from 111 to NNN enters at most once as well as leaves the bus at most once.

Determine whether the sequence of events is consistent or not (i.e. no person leaves the bus before entering and the number of passengers in the bus does not exceed MMM at any point of time).


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains Q+1Q + 1Q+1 lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N,M,QN, M, QN,M,Q.
QQQ lines follow. For each valid jjj, jthj^{th}jth of these lines contains a character chchch, followed by a space and an integer iii. Here chchch is either '+++' or '−-−' and 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.
It is guaranteed that ‘‘+``+‘‘+ i"i"i" and ‘‘−``-‘‘− i"i"i" appears at most once for every 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one string - "Consistent" (without quotes) if the sequence is consistent, "Inconsistent" (without quotes) otherwise.
constraints: Each person i appears at most once with '+' and at most once with '-'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains three integers N, M and Q — the number of people, the bus capacity and the number of events. Then Q lines follow, each containing a character '+' or '-' and an integer i (1 ≤ i ≤ N) separated by a space. '+' i means person i enters the bus, '-' i means person i leaves the bus.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing either "Consistent" if the whole sequence of events can happen without any person leaving before entering and without the number of passengers ever exceeding the capacity M, otherwise output "Inconsistent".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: after person 2 enters, the bus has 2 passengers while capacity is 1 → inconsistent. Test case 2 respects both rules.
Test case 1: person 3 leaves before entering. Test case 2 never exceeds capacity and no illegal leave.

hidden.label: all enter, none leave, within capacity

solution.approach: We simulate the process. Keep a boolean array inside[1..N] (or a hash set) indicating whether a person is currently on the bus and a counter cur for the current number of passengers. For each event: • If it is '+ i' and inside[i] is true, the sequence is already invalid (duplicate entry). Otherwise set inside[i]=true, increment cur and check if cur > M – if so, the sequence is inconsistent. • If it is '- i' and inside[i] is false, a person tries to leave before entering → inconsistent. Otherwise set inside[i]=false and decrement cur. If we finish all events without detecting a violation, the sequence is consistent.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q) per test case, because each event is processed in O(1). Overall O(T·Q).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the boolean array (or O(N) for a hash set).
